traders.strategy_detail: The dominant weather trader on Polymarket. gopfan2 is one of two wallets operated by the same trader (the other being 'gopfan', rank 4). Together they have extracted over $468K from weather markets. The operator bets large on global temperature anomaly markets — particularly 'hottest month on record' events — using what appears to be a solid understanding of climate science and NOAA/NASA data. Key wins include a $198,870 payout on the Global Heat Increase 2024 event and a $178,799 win on the January 2025 temperature anomaly market. Strategy: concentrated, high-conviction positions on well-researched climate data outcomes. Holds positions to resolution rather than trading in and out. The 'gopfan2' wallet appears to be a newer account used for larger positions after the original 'gopfan' wallet established the approach.

Second-ranked all-time with $288K PnL on over $10M in volume (efficiency ~2.9%). aenews2 is part of a cluster of at least three accounts (aenews, aenews2, aenews-915, aebonds) run by the same operator. The biggest single trade: $266,992 profit on '2024 July hottest on record?' with $415K wagered. aenews2 also won big on '2024 August hottest on record?' wagering $457K. Strategy: high-volume systematic betting on monthly global temperature records. The operator spreads capital across multiple wallets, likely for position-sizing flexibility or to stay under detection thresholds. Deep familiarity with climate anomaly markets. Often takes YES on 'hottest on record' markets ahead of official data releases.

Third overall with $131K PnL on a massive $10.5M in volume (efficiency ~1.24%). ColdMath is a high-volume market participant who trades across many weather contracts simultaneously. The low efficiency ratio suggests either a market-making approach (providing liquidity and earning small edges), a hedging strategy across multiple correlated positions, or systematic losses offset by larger wins. Despite the low efficiency, consistency and volume make ColdMath one of the most active weather traders. Strategy: broad-market systematic trading with a focus on temperature anomaly and global heat index markets. Likely uses quantitative models informed by meteorological forecasts.

The original 'gopfan' wallet — older account of the same operator behind rank-1 gopfan2. With $118K PnL on only $739K volume (efficiency ~16%), this is one of the most efficient top-10 wallets. Key win: $159K payout on '2024 July hottest on record?'. The high efficiency suggests this wallet was used for smaller, more selective positions while gopfan2 scaled up. Strategy identical to gopfan2: climate-data-informed betting on temperature record events, concentrating capital on high-confidence outcomes.

$86.6K PnL on $410K volume = 21.1% efficiency — outstanding. bama124's biggest single trade was $388,683 returned on '2024 July hottest on record?' (wagered $303,877 for an ~$85K profit). This is a classic high-conviction, single-trade approach. The trader identified the July 2024 heat anomaly early and bet heavily. Strategy: opportunistic, concentrated betting on high-probability climate events. Very selective — few trades but large positions when conviction is high. Likely monitors NWS/NOAA monthly temperature data closely.

$81K PnL on $7.1M volume (efficiency ~1.14%). Hans323 is a very high-volume trader with an active X presence under the same handle. The low efficiency relative to volume is consistent with a market-making or liquidity-providing style, or systematic trading across many small weather positions. Active across a wide range of weather contract types. Strategy: broad systematic participation in weather markets, likely using automated or semi-automated order flow to capture small edges at high volume.

$71K on ~$953K volume (7.5% efficiency). A solid mid-volume sharp trader. The name suggests a retail participant who became a specialist in weather markets. Strategy: moderate-conviction weather market trading, likely focused on temperature anomaly and seasonal weather events, with above-average accuracy on long-horizon climate calls.

Strategy: high-frequency or high-volume systematic trading, possibly using weather markets as a hedge or complement to political/macro positions. Razor-thin but consistent edge across enormous volume.

The name says it all — this is an automated system. $64.9K on $2.57M volume (2.5% efficiency). Uses AI/ML models to identify mispriced weather contracts. Strategy: algorithmic detection of pricing inefficiencies in weather markets, likely integrating live meteorological data feeds (NWS, ECMWF, GFS model outputs) with Polymarket price data to find arb opportunities. Consistent, machine-driven execution. One of very few wallets to explicitly self-identify as a trading bot.

Combines meteorological intuition with community intelligence from X/Twitter weather forecasting circles. Regular activity across multiple market types.
